one_liner: Coda offers a free, self-serve REST API (Bearer token auth) covering docs/tables/rows/automations, with several community MCP servers already available, making agent toolkits buildable now.

auth_methods.notes: Bearer token generated from account API settings (coda.io/account/apiSettings), used as Authorization: Bearer {token} header.

self_serve_notes: API is free to use on any plan (free or paid workspace). Developer self-generates API token instantly from account settings; some actions require Doc Maker/Admin role within workspace.

api_surface.notes: Coda REST API v1 covers docs, tables, rows, columns, formulas, controls, automations; OpenAPI spec published.

evidence.supports: Bearer token auth, self-serve API token generation from account settings
Self-serve API key generation from coda.io/account, example usage
OpenAPI spec confirming broad REST API surface

flags: Community-built MCP servers exist but no official first-party MCP from Coda
Some API endpoints require Doc Maker/Admin role, limiting basic Editor tokens
